title: M27 Decal-on-grid renderer primitive
summary: ECS-native decal layer for flat textured overlays on a grid: blast-prediction overlays, danger zones, footprints, telegraphs. Component shape: `Decal { texture, gridCell|worldPos, layer, lifetimeMs?, colorTint? }`. Renderer keeps it as a small instanced quad mesh so 100+ decals stay cheap on WebGPU and WebGL. Required by Kaboom Crew's one-hit-threat readability (blast predict before fuse expires) and by future hazard / sector-modifier visualisation. Stays out of `examples/kaboom-crew/` because every grid game (roguelike, RTS, tower defense) needs the same primitive.
doneCriteria: `schemas/components/decal.schema.json` ships with the Decal component shape.
Engine system (`DecalSystem` under `engine/render/systems/`) renders a tagged set of decals as one instanced bucket per material profile.
Per-decal lifetime expires the entity automatically (re-uses Tween / TimeBased machinery).
Doctor's renderer-pool section shows decal bucket counts.
Kaboom Crew (or batch-bench) demo scene proves 200 decals run without measurable frame-time impact on a modern laptop GPU.
notes: Not the same as three.js DecalGeometry — that one projects onto arbitrary meshes; we want flat ground-plane quads, much cheaper.
